title: vsCRT Configuration
description: Session tree and per-node settings for the vsCRT VS Code extension. Normally lives at ~/.vscrt/vscrtConfig.json and is managed via the Connection view — but it's plain JSON and safe to edit by hand.

$schemaVersion.description: Internal schema version. Stamped by vsCRT's migration runner on save; never edit by hand.

folder.description: Top-level folders. Each folder holds servers and optional nested subfolders.

knownFingerprints.description: Pre-shared SHA-256 fingerprints for known-good hosts. The TOFU modal is skipped when the live scan matches; mismatches are surfaced so a rotated or compromised host is never silently accepted.

launchProfiles.description: Named sets of servers that launch together — for cross-folder morning/pre-deploy/post-incident routines.

vsCRT.doubleClickTerminalLocation.description: Where the SSH terminal opens when you double-click a server in the Connection view. Defaults to 'editor'. A node's 'terminalLocation' field overrides this.

vsCRT.buttonClickTerminalLocation.description: Where the SSH terminal opens when you invoke Connect from a context menu entry or the Command Palette. Defaults to 'panel'. A node's 'terminalLocation' field overrides this.

name.description: Display name. Must be unique among siblings. Cannot contain '/'.

icon.description: Codicon name without the 'codicon-' prefix. Examples: 'folder', 'folder-library', 'organization', 'cloud'. See https://microsoft.github.io/vscode-codicons/ for the full list.

subfolder.description: Nested subfolders. Renders with the 'folder-library' icon by default.

nodes.description: Servers that live directly in this folder.

endpoint.description: SSH target. Preferred format: user@host or user@host:port — e.g. 'deploy@prod-web:2201'. If no '@' is present, vsCRT falls back to the 'user' and 'hostName' fields.

icon.description: Codicon name without the 'codicon-' prefix. Examples: 'server', 'database', 'cloud', 'terminal-bash', 'terminal-linux', 'terminal-powershell'.

hostName.description: Optional hostname. Only used when 'endpoint' is not in user@host form.

user.description: Optional SSH user. Only used when 'endpoint' is not in user@host form.

preferredAuthentication.description: Authentication method. 'password' uses sshpass with the 'password' field; 'publickey' uses the 'identityFile' field. If omitted, vsCRT prompts interactively.

identityFile.description: Path to the PRIVATE SSH key (publickey auth only). '~' is expanded to the user's home directory. Must not end in '.pub'.

extraArgs.description: Raw extra arguments appended to the ssh command (e.g. '-v' or '-o KexAlgorithms=curve25519-sha256'). Injected BEFORE the default -o StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new so these win. Treated as a raw shell fragment — be careful with quoting.

jumpHost.description: SSH ProxyJump spec: '[user@]host[:port]', comma-separated for multi-hop chains (e.g. 'alice@bastion1,bob@bastion2'). Rendered as 'ssh -o ProxyJump=<this>'. Works with both password and publickey auth. Constrained to the safe character set for shell embedding; use 'extraArgs' if you need anything more exotic.

portForwards.description: Port forwards to establish alongside the session. Each entry is rendered as a raw '-L', '-R', or '-D' argument.

env.description: Environment variables set in the spawned terminal before ssh runs. Keys must be valid env-var names. Values are strings.

password.description: Password handle. '@secret:<uuid>' is an OS keychain reference (default). 'enc:v4:t=…,m=…,p=…:<iv>:<ct>' is an in-file Argon2id + AES-GCM ciphertext. Legacy 'enc:v3:…' is still readable. Plain text is auto-migrated into the configured backend on first load — don't hand-edit plaintext here.

passwordDelivery.description: How the password reaches sshpass. 'tempfile' (default on all platforms) writes a mode-0600 temp file with a trap to delete on exit. 'pipe' uses a token-authed loopback socket (Unix) or named pipe (Windows). 'argv' is the legacy last-resort path and can leak via `ps`.

passwordStorage.description: Where the password lives. 'secretstorage' (default) keeps only an '@secret:<uuid>' reference in this file; the actual password lives in the OS keychain. 'passphrase' encrypts with Argon2id + AES-GCM and stores the ciphertext inline — portable across machines.

terminalLocation.description: Per-node override for where the SSH terminal opens. Wins over the top-level vsCRT.*TerminalLocation settings.

agentForwarding.description: Emit 'ssh -A' so local agent keys are forwarded to the remote session. Convenient but lets the remote host impersonate you to anything your agent can authenticate to; leave off unless you know you need it.

addKeysToAgent.description: Maps to '-o AddKeysToAgent=<value>'. When set, ssh adds freshly unlocked keys to the running agent for re-use across sessions.

recordSession.description: Per-node override for session recording. 'off' disables recording for this node; any non-off value overrides the global vsCRT.sessionRecording in the other direction. Default (unset) honours the global.

commands.description: Saved shell snippets. Each becomes a QuickPick entry under 'vsCRT: Run Command…' and is sent to the spawned terminal verbatim.

script.description: Raw shell command(s) — sent via terminal.sendText so shell features (pipes, quoting, heredocs) work as usual on the remote.

description.description: Optional one-liner shown as the QuickPick description.

connectTimeoutSeconds.description: Maps to 'ssh -o ConnectTimeout=<n>'. Maximum wait (in seconds) for the TCP handshake to complete.

serverAliveIntervalSeconds.description: Maps to 'ssh -o ServerAliveInterval=<n>'. Sends a keepalive through the encrypted channel every <n> seconds. 0 disables.

identitiesOnly.description: Maps to 'ssh -o IdentitiesOnly=yes|no'. When true, ssh only uses the identity files configured here (or in ~/.ssh/config), ignoring any keys offered by the agent.

extraSshDirectives.description: Escape hatch for ~/.ssh/config directives not natively modelled. Each key/value pair becomes '-o <Key>=<Value>' at connect time. Keys must be alphanumeric (a-zA-Z); values must not contain shell metacharacters.

port.description: DEPRECATED. Merge the port into 'endpoint' (e.g. 'user@host:2201'). This field is migrated to the endpoint on first load.
port.deprecationMessage: Deprecated. Merge the port into 'endpoint' (e.g. 'user@host:2201'); vsCRT migrates this field automatically on first load.
